news.title: PLTY Promises Income From Palantir’s Wild Ride, But the 70 Percent Yield Hides a Capital Erosion Problem
news.summary: YieldMax PLTR Option Income Strategy ETF (NYSEARCA:PLTY) sells a compelling story: harvest option premium from Palantir’s volatility and collect a distribution rate currently at 66% annualized. The problem is what sits underneath. PLTY’s most recent weekly payout was classified as 95% return of capital and just 5% income, and the fund’s share price has collapsed ... news.title: Millionaire Habits That Have Nothing to Do with Income
news.summary: Many people assume becoming wealthy is all about earning a big salary, but those who have acquired significant wealth often relied on more than income to get them there. Many millionaires build habits based on consistency, discipline, and smart decision-making, rather than focusing on luxury lifestyles or huge paychecks. Some wealthy people drive ordinary cars, ... Millionaire Habits That Have Nothing to Do with Income

news.title: U.S. Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise as Quick Middle East Peace Prospects Weaken
news.summary: U.S. Treasury yields and the DXY dollar index were rising as renewed escalation in the Middle East weakened hopes for a near-term peace agreement.  "Oil prices rebounded as expectations for a swift diplomatic resolution receded, reinforcing inflation concerns that have increasingly influenced markets in recent weeks," Exness' Li Xing said in a note.  "As a result, Treasury yields moved higher across the curve, supported by the view that persistent energy-driven price pressures could force the Federal Reserve to maintain a cautious stance for longer."

news.title: We’re Keeping Too Much Cash in Our Accounts These Days
news.summary: About $5.6 trillion or 10% of Americans’ liquid wealth is in low-yielding bank deposits.  Trillions more sit in money-market mutual funds.  Over any longer period, the opportunity cost of holding it is likely to exceed that of poor timing in the stock market.

news.title: Gold Falls to Two-Month Low as Middle East Tensions Fuel Inflation Concerns
news.summary: Gold prices moved lower on Thursday, slipping to their weakest levels in two months as renewed military tensions between the United States and Iran pushed oil prices higher and revived fears of a fresh inflation shock. At 05:33 ET (09:33 GMT), spot gold had fallen 1.

news.title: U.S. Futures Slip as Renewed U.S.-Iran Strikes Shake Market Sentiment: Dow Jones, S&P, Nasdaq, Wall Street
news.summary: U. S. equity futures traded lower on Thursday while oil prices climbed after a fresh exchange of military strikes between the United States and Iran undermined hopes for a near-term peace agreement.

news.title: Raoul Pal: How I Invest in Crypto
news.summary: In this solo presentation, Raoul Pal explains why crypto is a long-term network adoption story, how liquidity and the business cycle drive the asset class, and why trying to perfectly time the cycle can often do more harm than good. He also breaks down his approach to Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, Sui, altcoins, risk management, self-custody, and using volatility to compound over time. Recorded on May 21, 2026.

news.title: ByteDance making custom CPU chips to support AI rollout, sources say
news.summary:
STORY: Chinese tech giant ByteDance is developing its own central processing units (CPUs) to support its growing AI infrastructure needs, according to three sources.

The TikTok-parent's move comes as surging chip prices and prolonged supply shortages constrain its expansion plans.

It shows the industry's rapid shift towards autonomous AI models that demand more from CPUs.

Working in tandem with the dominant graphics chips made by Nvidia.

But it's created a shortage of CPUs in recent months.

A growing number of tech companies have concluded the economics of custom chips outweigh the complexity of designing them.

Global hyperscalers including Google, Amazon and Microsoft are also developing their own custom CPUs.

They aim to reduce costs and tailor to their specific workloads.

The first source said Bytedance is targeting deployment of its own CPU in its servers and data centers to support internal operations.

That's as it prepares a massive rollout of agent-based products.

The Beijing-based company has approached several external partners to assist with the effort, the sources claim.

ByteDance did not respond to Reuters' request for comment.

news.title: Marvell Stock Drops After Earnings Beat. It Sees Growth in Powering the AI Boom.
news.summary: The data-center boom is still going strong for  Marvell Technology  The chip company reported better-than-expected first-quarter revenue and sees higher revenue than expected in the current quarter.  Marvell stock dropped 2.6% in Thursday’s premarket following the results.  The company reported revenue of $2.42 billion, compared with Wall Street estimates of $2.41 billion, according to FactSet.
